summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that public flows can be executed without authentication, leading to arbitrary code execution via the PythonREPL component. The exploit successfully created a PUBLIC flow with malicious code and triggered execution without any authentication credentials, only requiring an arbitrary client_id cookie.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should create a PUBLIC flow with PythonREPL component, trigger execution via the unauthenticated public endpoint, and produce a proof file at /tmp/evohunt_public_pwned.txt containing the success marker string.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction successfully: (1) started the Langflow server with default configuration, (2) created a PUBLIC flow containing PythonREPL component with malicious code, (3) triggered flow execution via POST /api/v1/chat/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow without authentication (only client_id cookie), (4) executed arbitrary Python code that wrote a proof file, (5) verified the proof file contained the expected success marker.
verification_method: The exploit script checks for the existence of /tmp/evohunt_public_pwned.txt and verifies it contains the exact proof string 'EVOHUNT_PUBLIC_FLOW_POC_SUCCESS'. This distinguishes successful code execution from flow execution errors, authentication failures, or component initialization issues. The use of a fresh session with only a client_id cookie proves no authentication was required.
evidence_produced_by_run: PUBLIC flow with PythonREPL component was successfully created via authenticated API
Public flow execution endpoint accepted POST request without authentication credentials
Only client_id cookie was required (no JWT token or API key)
Python code was executed inside the Langflow container
Proof file /tmp/evohunt_public_pwned.txt was created with expected content
Flow marked as PUBLIC was accessible to unauthenticated users

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the Langflow repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
